lemonde:
LIVE, war in the Middle East: more than 210 dead in Lebanon; Donald Trump not hostile to a cleric at the head of Iran. 
Around 300,000 people have been displaced in Lebanon, according to the NGO Norwegian Refugee Council, due to Israeli bombing targeting the southern suburbs of Beirut and southern Lebanon. For his part, the American president demanded “unconditional surrender” from Iran.

In the Strait of Hormuz, the story of sailors stranded and in danger. 
VIDEO In the Strait of Hormuz, visual stories of sailors stranded and in danger

Death of Quentin Deranque: indictment of the two men arrested on Wednesday. 
Aged 22 and 26, they were arrested Wednesday morning in the Lyon region and Aube, two weeks after a first wave of eleven arrests.

LIVE, war in Ukraine: Volodymyr Zelensky visited the Donetsk region, close to the front line. 
The Ukrainian president filmed himself in a square in the town of Druzhkivka, around fifteen kilometers from the front.

At the 2026 Paralympic Games, a “tight” French delegation but full of ambition. 
Nearly half of the 13 selected for this Italian edition have already appeared on a podium at the Games, and four of them are even reigning champions. France is represented in four of the six disciplines on the program.

Trial of Tariq Ramadan for rape: an arrest warrant issued by France. 
The preacher is in Switzerland and has still not appeared for his trial which opened on Monday. Two doctors have ruled that he is fit to stand trial.

Hélène Mouchard-Zay, tireless fighter for the memory of the victims of the Vichy regime, is dead. 
Daughter of Jean Zay, Minister of the Popular Front assassinated by the Militia in 1944, she worked to have her ashes transferred to the Pantheon in 2015. She died on March 2 at the age of 85.

Alexis Spire, sociologist: “Among the working and middle classes, belief in the capacity of the State to protect them is wavering.” 
The specialist in inequalities studies the difficult relations between the French and public institutions. He explains, in an interview with “Le Monde”, that distrust is not the fruit of the rise of populism, but results from the inability of the State to meet the expectations of citizens.

Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa, loyal ally of Donald Trump. 
Ecuador and the United States announced the establishment of “joint operations” to fight against “terrorist organizations in the country”.

With the album “Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally,” Harry Styles is putting his pop star title back on the line. 
Highly anticipated, since the triumph of “Harry’s House” (2022), the Englishman’s fourth solo record takes an electronic route, with more or less success.

In Formula 1, the cards are being reshuffled after the major upheaval of the new regulations. 
Driving completely renewed single-seaters, due to a change in rules, F1 drivers launch the 2026 season on Sunday at the Australian Grand Prix. Despite the uncertainties, McLaren aspires to retain its titles against Mercedes, Red Bull and Ferrari.

Municipal 2026: in Saint-Colomban-des-Villards, in Savoie, free skiing may not survive the elections. 
At a time when climate change is profoundly changing winters, the town located at an altitude of 1,150 meters has made its three slopes freely accessible and wants to move away from the all-ski economy. An orientation called into question by the sole candidate for mayor, who fears a loss of dynamism of the station.

In “Celeste”, on Arte.tv, the actress Carmen Machi, revealed by “La Mesias”, is astonishing as Tax Columbo. 
The Spanish actress takes on the role of an inspector responsible for bringing down an international pop star, Celeste, played by Andrea Bayardo.

“We also see sex scenes filmed”: when Meta glasses subcontractors in Nairobi see everything, really everything. 
Two Swedish media outlets have revealed that employees of a Meta subcontractor in Kenya are viewing images captured by “Ray-Ban Meta” smart glasses, including intimate scenes. A complaint was filed in the United States.

The death of the “Dawson” actor or the end of a fantasy America. 
“Total Screen.” The premature death of James Van Der Beek, famous for the role of Dawson in the series of the same name, follows those of several actors who left their mark on television in the 1990s. With them, an entire era disappears. And the image of a certain America.

Peru condemned for the death of a woman during a forced sterilization campaign. 
The Inter-American Court of Human Rights found the country responsible for the forced sterilization and death of Celia Ramos in 1997, an emblematic case of a practice that affected more than 300,000 women under President Alberto Fujimori.

Discover ancient history by getting off the beaten track. 
The latest issue of the quarterly journal “Annales” presents new advances in research on Antiquity.
mediapart:
Islamologist Tariq Ramadan will be tried in absentia in Paris for rape, an arrest warrant issued against him. 
Swiss Islamologist Tariq Ramadan will be tried for rape in absentia and behind closed doors, the Paris departmental criminal court decided on Friday, issuing an arrest warrant for the accused who has not appeared before French justice since the opening of his trial.

“Mamadou”, “Bordeaux or Yemen?”: the RN candidate in Pau tries to forget her racist posts. 
The head of the far-right party list in Pau, Margaux Taillefer, has deleted several racist, LGBTphobic and fatphobic publications posted in recent years on the social network X. This former Zemmourist is now seeking to smooth out her image.

Municipal: the trial by fire for Politicae, the school of mayors financed by Pierre-Édouard Stérin. 
The training institute supported by the ultraconservative billionaire hopes to see its students win thousands of town halls in municipal elections. Several candidates supported by Politicae, however, are distancing themselves from the reactionary agenda of Pierre-Édouard Stérin.

Lebanon and Iran under bombs, Trump demands “unconditional surrender” from Iran. 
Donald Trump demanded the “unconditional surrender” of Iran after a week of war, explosions once again shaking Tehran and Lebanon on Friday, causing a mass exodus of residents of the southern suburbs of Beirut.

Ziad Majed on Lebanon: “Israel is in a logic of total war”. 
As Hezbollah supports Iran, Israel “took the opportunity to widen the conflict” and is now threatening to strike Beirut. Researcher and political scientist Ziad Majed discusses the new issues emerging in Lebanon.

“Public media are under attack almost everywhere in Europe.” 
Public broadcasting is facing a drastic drop in its resources, when it is not threatened with political takeover. For Christina Holtz-Bacha, professor emeritus in communication sciences, it is urgent to reaffirm its missions of general interest.

In Switzerland, public broadcasting endangered by a vote. 
Swiss voters must decide whether they want to reduce the license fee and cut almost half of the public broadcasting budget. A new episode of the culture war which endangers the diversity of Switzerland.

Epstein files: Justice Department releases testimony accusing Donald Trump. 
New documents were made public Thursday March 5. Three are particularly explosive because they directly implicate the Republican president. A woman accuses him of raping her in the 1980s, when she was a minor.

Despair grips the displaced people of Beirut, left to their own devices. 
The day after an Israeli order for a mass evacuation of the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, families taking refuge in the city center fear a long war and “indiscriminate strikes”. “The United States and Israel are lost, and they are losing us with them,” says a resident.

Disability: La Poste sentenced to 100,000 euros for discrimination against an employee. 
After more than five years of being put on the shelf, a former postwoman, recognized as a disabled worker, had La Poste condemned and her dismissal canceled. But his reinstatement, pronounced in June, is still not effective.
